statute: Nevada Revised Statutes Chapter 624 (Nevada State Contractors Board)
license_label: Nevada State Contractors Board License Number
license_notes: Nevada requires ALL contractors to carry a state license. Residential GC = Class B; specialty/subs = Class C. Unlicensed contracting is illegal (1st offense minor misdemeanor, 3rd offense Class E felony). No lien rights for unlicensed contractors.

mandatory_clauses.label: NSCB license disclosure
Right to cancel (home solicitation)
mandatory_clauses.text:
RIGHT TO CANCEL: You have the right to cancel this contract within three business days. To cancel, send written notice to:
{{contractor_name}}, {{contractor_address}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: 1st offense minor misdemeanor. 2nd offense gross misdemeanor. 3rd offense Class E felony. Fines $1,000-$50,000. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors.
notes: Nevada requires ALL contractors to hold a state license from the NSCB. Class B (residential GC), Class C (specialty/subs). Bond required ($1,000-$500,000 depending on license). Unlicensed contracting is a serious crime that escalates to a felony on 3rd offense. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors. Federal 3-day notice satisfies state cancellation requirements.
